question: Is the Chinese name '张伟' and the title '高级设计师' clearly visible on the inserted paper card?
Is the blue geometric logo visible on the left side of the paper card, consistent with the reference image?
Are fingerprint oil smudges visible on the surface of the acrylic holder, matching the style of the reference image?
Is the inserted nameplate content (text and logo) perspective-warped to match the angle, tilt, and slight curvature of the acrylic holder?
Does the inserted paper card appear to be physically located *inside* the acrylic holder (exhibiting slight refraction, soft focus, or matte finish compared to the glossy surface)?
Do the fingerprint smudges appear to be on the *outer* acrylic surface, layering over the text and paper content beneath?
Are the fingerprint smudges positioned primarily near or within the highlight/reflection areas of the acrylic surface?
Is the venetian blind light stripe pattern continuous across the nameplate holder, matching the direction and style of the original scene?
Are the light stripes on the outer acrylic surface distinctly sharper and more pronounced than the stripes on the paper card inside?
Do the light stripes on the inserted paper card appear softer and more diffused, consistent with being viewed through acrylic?
Is the wooden table surface and its lighting pattern preserved without unintended alterations?
Is the laptop in the background preserved in its original position and appearance?
Is the structural shape of the acrylic holder (L-stand, edges) preserved without distortion or melting artifacts?
Is the inserted text legible and free from severe distortion, blurring, or character errors?
Is the image free from visible compositing artifacts such as hard cutout seams, unnatural color banding, or inconsistent noise?
